brief_summary: Three-column feature matrix — QMK / VIA / VIAL — across six capability rows: GUI remap, layer count, tap-dance, combos, macros, and leader/OLED/host-aware behavior. Amber circles for supported, em-dash for unsupported, numerical or word values where the capability is partial. Caption: 'QMK is the floor everything else stands on'.
data_sources: keyboard-firmware-compared.mdx § 'QMK' — 'Layers, tap-dance, Mod-Tap, Combos, Leader sequences, one-shot modifiers, dynamic macros, RGB control, OLED rendering' all listed as in-codebase
keyboard-firmware-compared.mdx § 'VIA' — 'no Combo editor, no tap-dance editor, no Leader sequences. The keycode set in the GUI is a subset of QMK's'
keyboard-firmware-compared.mdx § 'VIAL' — 'VIAL supports tap-dance editing in the GUI. It supports Combos. It supports more layers (up to 16 versus VIA's 4)'
QMK upstream uses MAX_LAYER bitfield of 32 — referenced as 32 in matrix
warnings: QMK layer count of 32 reflects QMK's MAX_LAYER bitfield default upstream; the article cites only 'more layers' without a specific number, so this is a known-spec annotation rather than article-derived
